description: Complete the Phase 1 database foundation and shared infrastructure. Plans 01-03 through 01-06: auth hook, production_status REVOKE, workstation lifecycle functions, Supabase clients, proxy.ts, rate limiters, Sentry, auth module + UI, pgTAP suite, CI, and seed scripts.

userStories.title: Custom access token hook migration (0007)
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need the Supabase custom access token hook to stamp tenant_id, audience, and role into JWT app_metadata so that every authenticated request carries the correct tenant context without additional DB lookups.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/migrations/0007_auth_hook.sql exists
app.custom_access_token_hook function is declared STABLE
Function body contains zero INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statements
Function reads from staff, workstations, and customer_users (in that order) to determine audience
Function stamps tenant_id, audience ('staff_office' | 'staff_shop' | 'customer'), and role into event.claims.app_metadata
GRANT EXECUTE on app.custom_access_token_hook to supabase_auth_admin is present
REVOKE EXECUTE on app.custom_access_token_hook from authenticated, anon, public is present
GRANT SELECT on staff, workstations, customer_users to supabase_auth_admin is present
No ALTER ROLE statement touches supabase_auth_admin (BYPASSRLS must remain intact)
supabase db reset applies all migrations 0001-0007 without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL: Hook deadlock (Supabase Issue #29073) — any write inside the hook causes a deadlock. This is a hidden invariant in CLAUDE.md. Do NOT add INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE under any circumstances. Read CLAUDE.md hidden invariants and 01-RESEARCH.md Pitfall 1 before writing this migration.

userStories.title: production_status column-level REVOKE migration (0008)
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need direct UPDATE of jobs.production_status to be forbidden at the database level so that all stage transitions must go through app.record_scan_event() and are always auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/migrations/0008_production_status_revoke.sql exists
Migration contains: REVOKE UPDATE (production_status) ON jobs FROM authenticated
Running UPDATE jobs SET production_status = 'prep' as the authenticated role returns a permission error
supabase db reset applies all migrations 0001-0008 without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Single-line migration. Keep it minimal — do not add anything else to this file.

userStories.title: Workstation lifecycle SECURITY DEFINER functions migration (0009)
userStories.description: As a workstation tablet, I need SECURITY DEFINER functions to claim a workstation session, record heartbeats, and release the session so that workstation identity is managed safely without exposing the workstations table to direct writes.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/migrations/0009_workstation_lifecycle_functions.sql exists
app.claim_workstation(p_device_token TEXT) RETURNS UUID exists as SECURITY DEFINER
app.record_workstation_heartbeat() RETURNS VOID exists as SECURITY DEFINER
app.release_workstation() RETURNS VOID exists as SECURITY DEFINER
Each function validates that the caller's app.workstation_id() matches the row being modified
claim_workstation verifies the device_token bcrypt hash against the stored hash
supabase db reset applies all migrations 0001-0009 without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read DESIGN.md §3.2 for the exact function signatures. These functions must not expose raw workstation credentials.

userStories.title: link_auth_user_to_actor trigger migration (0010)
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need an AFTER INSERT trigger on auth.users to automatically link new auth users to their pre-created staff or customer_users row by tenant_id and email so that the hook can find them on first sign-in.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/migrations/0010_link_auth_user_trigger.sql exists
app.link_auth_user_to_actor() SECURITY DEFINER function exists
AFTER INSERT trigger on auth.users fires app.link_auth_user_to_actor
Function reads app_metadata.tenant_id from NEW.raw_app_meta_data to scope the search
Function raises exception 'auth_user_created_without_tenant_id' if app_metadata.tenant_id is missing
Function links staff row (by tenant_id + email) if found; else links customer_users row
Function does NOT run inside the custom_access_token_hook (it is a separate trigger)
supabase db reset applies all migrations 0001-0010 without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read 01-RESEARCH.md Pitfall 5. This trigger fires on auth.users INSERT (e.g. inviteUserByEmail), NOT inside the hook. The hook is read-only; this trigger is the write path.

userStories.title: Supabase database clients in src/shared/db/
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed Supabase clients (server, browser, admin) in src/shared/db/ so that all database access is consistently typed and the service-role client is gated by ESLint.
acceptanceCriteria: src/shared/db/server.ts exports async createClient() using await cookies() from next/headers, returns SupabaseClient<Database>
src/shared/db/client.ts exports createBrowserClient() for browser-side subscriptions
src/shared/db/admin.ts exports createServiceClient() using SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (not NEXT_PUBLIC_*)
src/shared/db/types.ts exports a placeholder Database type (to be replaced by pnpm gen:types after Plan 06)
src/shared/db/index.ts re-exports all three clients
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes (no ESLint violations for these files)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: NEVER use getSession() anywhere in these files — always getUser(). cookies() from next/headers is async; always await it. Read CLAUDE.md stack constraints before writing.

userStories.title: Auth helper guards and claims accessor in src/shared/auth-helpers/
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed auth guard functions (requireOfficeStaff, requireShopStaff, requireCustomer) and a getCurrentClaims() helper so that Server Components and Server Actions can enforce audience in a single call.
acceptanceCriteria: src/shared/auth-helpers/require.ts exports requireOfficeStaff(), requireShopStaff(), requireCustomer()
Each guard calls supabase.auth.getUser() (never getSession())
Each guard reads audience from JWT app_metadata (via getCurrentClaims()) and redirects to /sign-in on mismatch
src/shared/auth-helpers/claims.ts exports getCurrentClaims() returning typed { tenant_id, audience, role, staff_id?, workstation_id?, company_id? }
src/shared/auth-helpers/index.ts re-exports both files
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read DESIGN.md §4.4 for the exact function signatures expected by downstream modules. Guards redirect (not throw) on failure.

userStories.title: src/proxy.ts multi-domain routing and session refresh
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need proxy.ts to inspect the Host header and enforce that office JWTs cannot access the portal domain (and vice versa) so that audience isolation is enforced at the routing layer.
acceptanceCriteria: src/proxy.ts exists (NOT middleware.ts — Next.js 16 uses proxy.ts)
File reads NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_HOST and NEXT_PUBLIC_PORTAL_HOST env vars for domain comparison
Customer JWT on app.* domain redirects to track.* sign-in
Office/shop JWT on track.* domain redirects to app.* sign-in
Supabase session is refreshed on every request using @supabase/ssr
Near-expiry workstation sessions are silently re-authenticated (1-hour TTL mitigation)
Cookie domain attribute is never set (host-scoped only, no cross-subdomain bleed)
Rate limiting applied to /sign-in and /magic-link paths using signInLimiter and magicLinkLimiter from src/shared/rate-limit/
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read DESIGN.md §102 and 01-RESEARCH.md for proxy.ts requirements. The file must be named proxy.ts not middleware.ts. Read CLAUDE.md stack constraints.

userStories.title: Upstash rate limiters in src/shared/rate-limit/
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need sliding-window rate limiters for sign-in and magic-link endpoints so that brute-force and enumeration attacks are blocked at both the proxy and Server Action layers.
acceptanceCriteria: src/shared/rate-limit/index.ts exports signInLimiter and magicLinkLimiter
Both limiters use @upstash/ratelimit sliding-window algorithm
signInLimiter: 5 attempts per hour per IP+email
magicLinkLimiter: 3 attempts per hour per email
src/shared/rate-limit/adapter.ts exports a getIdentifier(req) helper for extracting the rate-limit key
.env.local.example contains UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL and UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN placeholders
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Rate limiters are consumed by BOTH proxy.ts (proxy layer) and Server Actions (defense-in-depth). Do not implement only in one place.

userStories.title: Sentry initialization with tenant_id tagging
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need Sentry initialized on client, server, and edge so that errors are captured and tagged with tenant_id from the JWT for cross-tenant observability.
acceptanceCriteria: instrumentation.ts registers Sentry on server and edge
sentry.client.config.ts initializes Sentry browser SDK
sentry.server.config.ts initializes Sentry server SDK
sentry.edge.config.ts initializes Sentry edge SDK
Every Sentry event is tagged with tenant_id when available from JWT app_metadata
next.config.ts is wrapped with withSentryConfig()
.env.local.example contains SENTRY_DSN and SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN placeholders
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use @sentry/nextjs. Read the installed version from package.json (10.51.0). Tag tenant_id at the scope level so it propagates to all events in the request.

userStories.title: Shared infrastructure stubs (audit, realtime, storage)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need stub modules for src/shared/audit/, src/shared/realtime/, and src/shared/storage/ so that downstream modules can import from the canonical paths without hitting missing-module errors.
acceptanceCriteria: src/shared/audit/index.ts exports withAudit() HOF stub (body: TODO, returns the wrapped fn unchanged)
src/shared/realtime/index.ts exports a createRealtimeClient() stub
src/shared/storage/index.ts exports a getSignedUrl() stub
All stubs are typed (no `any`)
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stubs only — no real implementation. Mark each export with a TODO comment indicating which phase fills them in.

userStories.title: Office sign-in Server Action and sign-in page UI
userStories.description: As an office staff member, I want to sign in with my email and password at app.popscoating.com/sign-in so that I can access the CRM, jobs, and dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/auth/actions/sign-in.ts exports signInAction(formData: FormData) Server Action
Action calls supabase.auth.signInWithPassword() then redirects to /(office)/dashboard on success
Action validates email and password with Zod before calling Supabase
Action applies signInLimiter rate check before attempting auth
Action returns typed error on failure (invalid credentials, rate limit, network)
src/app/(office)/sign-in/page.tsx renders sign-in form using shadcn Form + Input + Button + Alert
Form shows error messages from the Server Action using next-intl t() keys from src/messages/en/auth.json
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read src/messages/en/auth.json for existing error string keys. Use only keys that exist — do not add new strings without updating the messages file.

userStories.title: Sign-out Server Action
userStories.description: As an office staff member or customer, I want to sign out so that my session is cleared and I am redirected to the sign-in page.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/auth/actions/sign-out.ts exports signOutAction() Server Action
Action calls supabase.auth.signOut() then redirects to /sign-in for the current domain
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep this minimal. The redirect target should use the proxy.ts domain logic.

userStories.title: Portal magic-link Server Action, callback route, and portal sign-in UI
userStories.description: As a customer, I want to enter my email at track.popscoating.com/sign-in to receive a magic link so that I can access my job tracking portal without a password.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/auth/actions/request-magic-link.ts exports requestMagicLinkAction(formData: FormData) Server Action
Action applies magicLinkLimiter rate check before calling Supabase
Action calls supabase.auth.signInWithOtp() with redirectTo pointing to /api/auth/callback
Action always returns silent success regardless of whether the email exists (anti-enumeration)
src/app/api/auth/callback/route.ts exchanges the auth code for a session and redirects to /(portal)/jobs
src/app/(portal)/sign-in/page.tsx renders the magic-link request form using shadcn Form + Input + Button
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Silent success on unknown email is security-critical (anti-enumeration per §7.2). The callback route must handle both the code exchange and session cookie setting.

userStories.title: createWorkstation Server Action
userStories.description: As an office staff admin, I want a Server Action to enroll a new workstation tablet so that the tablet gets a synthetic Supabase user and a device_token.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/auth/actions/create-workstation.ts exports createWorkstationAction(formData: FormData) Server Action
Action is guarded by requireOfficeStaff()
Action uses the service-role client (createServiceClient) to call auth.admin.createUser() with a synthetic email and device_token as password
Action creates a row in the workstations table with the new auth user's id, tenant_id, and label
Action returns the device_token (shown once to the admin for QR generation)
pnpm type-check passes
pnpm lint passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The device_token is a 48-char URL-safe random string. Generate with crypto.randomBytes(36).toString('base64url'). This is the workstation's password — shown once, never stored plaintext.

userStories.title: Wave 1 module stubs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need stub index.ts barrel files for all Wave 1 modules so that ESLint module-boundary rules can be validated and downstream plans can import from modules without missing-module errors.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/auth/index.ts exists and re-exports the auth actions
src/modules/crm/index.ts exists with a stub export
src/modules/jobs/index.ts exists with a stub export
src/modules/packets/index.ts exists with a stub export
src/modules/scanning/index.ts exists with a stub export
src/modules/timeline/index.ts exists with a stub export
src/modules/dashboard/index.ts exists with a stub export
src/modules/portal/index.ts exists with a stub export
src/modules/settings/index.ts exists with a stub export
src/modules/tags/index.ts exists with a stub export
pnpm lint passes (madge:check finds no circular imports)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stubs only — each index.ts can export a single comment or a placeholder type. The point is to satisfy ESLint no-restricted-imports rules that require cross-module imports to go through index.ts.

userStories.title: pgTAP RLS test suite
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I need a pgTAP test suite covering cross-tenant isolation and audience-scoped access so that RLS regressions are caught in CI before they reach production.
acceptanceCriteria: supabase/tests/01_rls_tenant_isolation.sql exists
Test verifies that a query from Tenant A cannot return rows belonging to Tenant B on any business table
supabase/tests/02_rls_audience_scoped.sql exists
Test verifies that shop audience cannot INSERT into CRM tables
Test verifies that customer audience cannot INSERT into any table
supabase/tests/03_rls_customer_filters.sql exists
Test verifies that customer audience SELECT on jobs returns only rows where company_id matches app.company_id()
Test verifies that customer audience cannot see jobs with archived_at set
supabase/tests/04_rls_no_delete.sql exists
Test verifies that DELETE on any business table from authenticated role returns a permission error
pnpm supabase db reset && pnpm supabase test db passes with all tests green
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read 01-RESEARCH.md for pgTAP patterns. Each test file should set_config role and JWT claims before running assertions. Use set_config('request.jwt.claims', ...) to mock the JWT.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a GitHub Actions workflow that runs type-check, lint, and pgTAP tests on every push so that regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists
Workflow triggers on push to main and pull_request
Workflow runs: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
Workflow runs: pnpm type-check
Workflow runs: pnpm lint
Workflow runs: supabase db reset && supabase test db (pgTAP suite)
Workflow uses Node.js 20 and pnpm (correct version from package.json)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use the official pnpm/action-setup and supabase/setup-cli GitHub Actions. Cache pnpm store between runs.

userStories.title: Tenant 1 seed script and local dev fixture data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a seed-tenant.ts script and a supabase/seed.sql file so that I can bootstrap Tenant 1 (Pops Industrial Coatings) in both production and local dev with one command.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/seed-tenant.ts exists and is executable via pnpm tsx scripts/seed-tenant.ts
Script creates a tenants row for Pops with a fixed UUID (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001)
Script creates a shop_settings row for Tenant 1
Script calls supabase.auth.admin.inviteUserByEmail() to create the first office staff user
Script is idempotent (running twice does not error or duplicate data)
supabase/seed.sql exists and seeds local dev with: 1 tenant, 1 staff user, 2 companies, 1 contact, 3 jobs at different production_status values
supabase db reset applies seed.sql automatically (seed file in expected Supabase location)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The seed-tenant.ts script uses the service-role client. Read DESIGN.md §3.2 for the Tenant 1 UUID. The seed.sql fixture data is for local development only — never committed with real credentials.
